subject: Computer Networking
title: Web3 and Decentralization
description: A short lesson to help you apply Web3 and Decentralization.

hook.text: Web1 was read-only. Web2 let users create content. Web3 promises user ownership via blockchain.

buildup.text: Web3 uses smart contracts on Ethereum and other chains. Apps run on decentralized networks, not company servers.

discovery.text: Users own their data and digital assets as tokens. No central authority controls access or content.

twist.text: In practice, Web3 relies on centralized services: Infura for nodes, OpenSea for NFTs, MetaMask for wallets.

climax.text: Scalability and UX remain major hurdles. Gas fees and seed phrases confuse mainstream users.

punchline.text: Web3 envisions a user-owned internet. The reality is still evolving.
